title: pouteria baranensis
intro: Pouteria baranensis, also known as the Koa haole or Hawaiian breadfruit, is a species of plant in the family Sapotaceae. It is a tropical tree native to Hawaii and other Pacific Island regions, recognized for its medicinal properties and edible fruit. The bark and leaves of the Pouteria baranensis plant are used in herbal preparations to treat a range of health issues, including fever, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's health benefits include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and provide antioxidant protection, making it a valuable resource in traditional Hawaiian medicine.
